label: Trigger
description: Workflow entry point. Emits the input as the first event so downstream nodes can reference it via {{trigger.body.*}} and {{trigger.<key>}}.

settings.name: triggerType
settings.label: Trigger type
settings.description: Source of the triggering event. 'manual' is the default for ad-hoc runs. 'workflow_finished' (P1.19) fires this flow when another flow completes — configure via triggerConfig: { sourceWorkflowId | sourceWorkflowSlug, matchStatus?: 'completed'|'failed'|'any' }. Inputs available downstream: {{trigger.sourceExecutionId}}, {{trigger.sourceWorkflowId}}, {{trigger.sourceStatus}}, {{trigger.sourceOutput}}.

settings.name: triggerConfig
settings.label: Trigger configuration
settings.description: Type-specific config (e.g. webhook path, cron expression, event topic).

ai.shortDescription: Workflow entry-point — emits its input as the first event.
ai.whenToUse: Always at the start of a flow. Place exactly one trigger node so {{trigger.body.*}} resolves downstream.
ai.examplePrompt: add a manual trigger that accepts { message, userId }
ai.promptHints: Reference the payload as {{trigger.body.<field>}} (canonical) or {{trigger.<field>}} (flat alias for object inputs).

outputAssertions.name: non_empty_content
outputAssertions.errorMessage: Trigger node received no input — workflow has nothing to template against.
